articles.headline: Tragedy Strikes IAF Day Celebrations in Chennai: Crowd Crush Claims Multiple Lives
articles.explainer:
• At least five individuals lost their lives, and many others were hospitalized due to severe dehydration and injuries sustained in a crowd crush.
• The incident occurred during the 92nd-anniversary celebrations of the Indian Air Force at Tambaram Air Force Station in Chennai, Tamil Nadu.
• Overcrowding, inadequate facilities, and insufficient crowd management are being cited as primary reasons for the stampede-like situation.
• Authorities have initiated an inquiry into the incident to ascertain lapses in planning and execution of the large-scale public event.
• The event, meant to showcase IAF's prowess, turned tragic, highlighting critical issues in public event safety and disaster preparedness.
gsSubTopics: Disaster Management
Role of security forces and agencies
Government policies and interventions
prelimsPoints: Location of incident: Tambaram Air Force Station, Chennai, Tamil Nadu.
Event: 92nd anniversary celebration of the Indian Air Force.
Cause: Crowd crush leading to dehydration and injuries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in crowd management during large public gatherings and suggest measures for effective disaster prevention.
Analyze the role of various stakeholders, including event organizers, local administration, and security forces, in ensuring public safety.
Examine the legal and ethical responsibilities in cases of public safety failures at government-organized events.
articles.imageDescription: Emergency services responding to a crowd crush incident at a public event.
articles.trivia: The Indian Air Force was officially established on October 8, 1932, as the Royal Indian Air Force.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Disaster Management
prelimsSyllabus.area: Disaster and disasters management in India
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Types of disasters and their management

articles.headline: India's G20 Presidency Legacy: A Year On, Assessing Impact and Future Trajectory
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive review is underway to assess the long-term impact and successful implementation of initiatives launched during India's G20 Presidency.
• Key focus areas include the New Delhi Leaders' Declaration, particularly commitments on sustainable development, climate finance, and digital public infrastructure.
• Discussions are centering on India's push for the inclusion of the African Union as a permanent G20 member and its implications for global governance.
• Experts are evaluating the effectiveness of the 'Voice of Global South Summit' in shaping the G20 agenda and promoting inclusive multilateralism.
• The review aims to identify enduring policy changes and frameworks that continue to guide global cooperation post-India's tenure.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: India held the G20 Presidency from December 1, 2022, to November 30, 2023.
The African Union became a permanent member of the G20 during India's presidency.
Key outcomes included the New Delhi Leaders' Declaration and emphasis on DPI.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the successes and challenges of India's G20 Presidency in advancing global cooperation and its national interests.
Discuss how India leveraged its G20 platform to champion the cause of the Global South and its implications for future international relations.
Analyze the significance of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) as a key deliverable of India's G20 Presidency and its potential for global replication.
articles.imageDescription: Logo of the G20 with India's national flower, the lotus.
articles.trivia: The G20 was formed in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis as a forum for finance ministers and central bank governors.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Review Compliance on Electoral Bonds Disclosure
articles.explainer:
• A Supreme Court bench is scheduled to review the compliance reports submitted by the Election Commission of India (ECI) regarding the disclosure of electoral bond data.
• The court had earlier struck down the Electoral Bond Scheme as unconstitutional, citing violations of the right to information and voter's right to know.
• The review will ascertain if all directions, including the complete disclosure of donor and recipient details, have been adhered to by the State Bank of India (SBI) and ECI.
• This ongoing scrutiny emphasizes the judiciary's commitment to transparency in political funding and democratic processes.
• The outcome could further shape future regulations on political donations and electoral reforms.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: The Supreme Court declared the Electoral Bond Scheme unconstitutional in February 2024.
The ruling emphasized the Right to Information (Article 19(1)(a)) of voters.
State Bank of India (SBI) was directed to disclose details of electoral bonds to ECI.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on electoral bonds for transparency in political funding and democratic accountability in India.
Discuss the challenges in regulating political finance in India and suggest reforms to ensure fair and free elections.
Examine the role of the judiciary in upholding constitutional values and safeguarding fundamental rights in matters of electoral reforms.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building facade.
articles.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held from October 1951 to February 1952.

articles.headline: India on Track to Achieve Ambitious Renewable Energy Targets by 2030
articles.explainer:
• Ministry of New and Renewable Energy reports significant progress towards India's target of 500 GW non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
• Recent policy initiatives and investment drives have accelerated solar and wind energy projects across the country.
• India's commitment to energy transition is crucial for meeting its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement.
• Challenges remain in grid integration, energy storage solutions, and land acquisition for large-scale projects.
• The government is exploring advanced technologies like green hydrogen and offshore wind to further diversify its renewable energy portfolio.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: India's target: 500 GW non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
Key renewable energy sources: Solar, Wind, Hydro.
India's NDCs are under the Paris Agreement.
mainsPoints: Discuss the opportunities and challenges for India in achieving its ambitious renewable energy targets by 2030.
Analyze the role of government policies and private sector investment in accelerating India's energy transition.
Examine the environmental and economic benefits of shifting towards a renewable energy-dominated power sector in India.
articles.imageDescription: A large solar power farm with multiple photovoltaic panels.
articles.trivia: India is the third-largest producer of renewable energy in the world.

articles.headline: India's Digital Payments Ecosystem Continues Explosive Growth, Drives Financial Inclusion
articles.explainer:
• Latest data indicates a sustained surge in digital payment transactions, particularly through UPI, reinforcing India's leadership in fintech.
• The growth is attributed to widespread adoption of smartphones, government initiatives like Jan Dhan-Aadhaar-Mobile (JAM) trinity, and innovative payment solutions.
• This expansion is significantly contributing to financial inclusion, bringing unbanked and underbanked populations into the formal financial system.
• Regulatory bodies are focusing on strengthening cybersecurity measures and ensuring robust infrastructure to support the increasing transaction volumes.
• The success of India's digital payment model is being studied globally for its potential to transform economies.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: UPI is a real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
JAM Trinity: Jan Dhan accounts, Aadhaar, Mobile numbers.
Digital payments contribute to financial inclusion.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of digital payments, especially UPI, in driving financial inclusion and transforming India's economy.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities associated with the rapid expansion of India's digital payments ecosystem.
Examine how India's success in digital public infrastructure can be replicated in other developing nations.
articles.imageDescription: A person making a payment using a smartphone with a UPI QR code.
articles.trivia: India's UPI system processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month for the first time in August 2023.

articles.headline: Gaganyaan Mission: ISRO Intensifies Preparations for Manned Spaceflight
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) is accelerating testing and training for the Gaganyaan mission, India's maiden manned spaceflight program.
• Key focus areas include astronaut training, development of life support systems, and validation of the crew module's re-entry capabilities.
• Recent uncrewed test flights have provided valuable data for refining mission parameters and ensuring crew safety.
• The mission aims to send Indian astronauts to low Earth orbit, marking a significant milestone in India's space endeavors.
• Collaboration with international space agencies continues for knowledge exchange and technical assistance.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Gaganyaan is India's first manned space mission.
ISRO is the agency responsible for the mission.
The mission aims to send astronauts to Low Earth Orbit (LEO).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance and scientific objectives of India's Gaganyaan mission.
Analyze the technological challenges and indigenous capabilities developed for the successful execution of manned spaceflight.
Examine the socio-economic benefits and international standing gained through ambitious space programs like Gaganyaan.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of the Gaganyaan crew module orbiting Earth.
articles.trivia: The first Indian to travel to space was Rakesh Sharma in 1984, aboard the Soviet Soyuz T-11.

articles.headline: National Logistics Policy: Driving Efficiency and Reducing Costs in India's Supply Chain
articles.explainer:
• The National Logistics Policy (NLP), launched in 2022, is showing tangible results in streamlining India's logistics sector.
• Efforts are focused on integrating digital systems, developing multi-modal transportation, and creating a unified regulatory framework.
• The policy aims to reduce logistics costs as a percentage of GDP, making Indian goods more competitive globally.
• Infrastructure projects like the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan are crucial enablers for the NLP's success.
• Improved logistics efficiency is expected to boost manufacturing, exports, and create new employment opportunities.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: National Logistics Policy (NLP) was launched in September 2022.
Aim: Reduce logistics cost as a percentage of GDP.
PM Gati Shakti is a complementary initiative for integrated infrastructure development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and key pillars of the National Logistics Policy and its potential to transform India's economic landscape.
Analyze how improved logistics can enhance India's manufacturing competitiveness and boost its position in global supply chains.
Examine the challenges in implementing a comprehensive logistics policy and suggest measures for effective coordination among various stakeholders.
articles.imageDescription: A container ship being loaded at a modern port, symbolizing logistics.
articles.trivia: India's logistics cost is estimated to be around 13-14% of its GDP, higher than the global average of 8-10%.

articles.headline: Delhi Government Unveils Winter Action Plan to Combat Air Pollution
articles.explainer:
• The Delhi government has announced a comprehensive action plan to tackle severe air pollution anticipated during the upcoming winter months.
• Measures include strict enforcement against stubble burning, vehicular emissions control, dust management, and industrial pollution monitoring.
• Focus on promoting public transport and electric vehicles, alongside green crackers during festivals.
• Inter-state cooperation with neighboring states like Punjab and Haryana remains crucial for addressing transboundary pollution sources.
• Public awareness campaigns and citizen participation are integral to the success of the anti-pollution efforts.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Key sources of air pollution in Delhi in winter: Stubble burning, vehicular emissions, dust, industrial pollution.
GRAP (Graded Response Action Plan) is implemented in Delhi-NCR to tackle air pollution.
CAQM (Commission for Air Quality Management) oversees air quality in NCR.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges in combating air pollution in Delhi-NCR and critically evaluate the effectiveness of current policy interventions.
Analyze the role of inter-state cooperation and technological solutions in addressing transboundary pollution issues like stubble burning.
Suggest sustainable long-term strategies for improving urban air quality, considering economic and social implications.
articles.imageDescription: A smoggy cityscape of Delhi during winter, highlighting air pollution.
articles.trivia: Delhi often ranks among the most polluted capital cities in the world, especially during winter.

articles.headline: India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEEC): Progress on Feasibility Studies
articles.explainer:
• Feasibility studies for the ambitious India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEEC) are progressing, with initial reports indicating strong economic potential.
• The corridor, announced at the G20 Summit, aims to enhance connectivity through railway and shipping networks, bypassing traditional routes.
• It is envisioned to boost trade, energy flow, and digital connectivity between India, the Middle East, and Europe.
• Geopolitical implications are significant, offering an alternative to China's Belt and Road Initiative (BRI).
• Member countries are actively engaging in discussions to finalize funding mechanisms and project timelines.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: IMEEC was announced during the G20 Summit in New Delhi (2023).
It connects India, the Middle East, and Europe through sea and rail routes.
It is seen as part of the Partnership for Global Infrastructure and Investment (PGII).
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEEC) for India's foreign policy and trade ambitions.
Compare and contrast IMEEC with China's Belt and Road Initiative (BRI), highlighting their respective geopolitical implications.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in the successful implementation of such a mega infrastructure project involving multiple nations.
articles.imageDescription: A map illustrating the proposed route of the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor.
articles.trivia: The idea for IMEEC emerged from discussions at the G7 Summit in 2023, preceding its G20 announcement.

articles.headline: India's Semiconductor Manufacturing Ambitions Gain Momentum with New Investments
articles.explainer:
• Several new proposals for semiconductor fabrication units and assembly, testing, marking, and packaging (ATMP) facilities are under government review.
• The 'India Semiconductor Mission' (ISM) is providing significant incentives and support to attract global players and foster indigenous capabilities.
• Aims to establish India as a global hub for semiconductor design and manufacturing, reducing reliance on imports.
• Challenges include capital intensity, technology transfer complexities, and skilled workforce availability.
• The strategic importance of semiconductors for national security and economic growth is driving this push.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) is a dedicated program to promote semiconductor manufacturing.
ATMP refers to Assembly, Testing, Marking, and Packaging, a critical part of chip manufacturing.
Semiconductors are essential components for modern electronics and digital infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic imperative for India to develop indigenous semiconductor manufacturing capabilities and the challenges involved.
Analyze the effectiveness of government policies, such as the India Semiconductor Mission, in attracting investments and fostering a semiconductor ecosystem.
Examine the potential impact of a robust domestic semiconductor industry on India's economic growth, technological sovereignty, and national security.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up of a silicon wafer used in semiconductor manufacturing.
articles.trivia: Globally, Taiwan and South Korea dominate the advanced semiconductor manufacturing market.

articles.headline: New Framework Proposed for Integrated Wetland Conservation and Management
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change is finalizing a new policy framework for the holistic conservation and management of India's wetlands.
• The framework emphasizes community participation, sustainable utilization of wetland resources, and integration with local development plans.
• Wetlands are vital ecosystems providing numerous services, including water purification, flood control, and biodiversity support.
• India is a signatory to the Ramsar Convention, which focuses on the conservation and wise use of wetlands.
• The initiative aims to protect and restore these critical habitats which are under increasing threat from urbanization and pollution.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and Environment.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Wetlands are transition zones between terrestrial and aquatic systems.
The Ramsar Convention (1971) is an international treaty for wetland conservation.
India has designated numerous Ramsar Sites, which are Wetlands of International Importance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of wetlands and the major threats they face in India.
Critically analyze the existing policy and legal framework for wetland conservation in India and suggest improvements.
Examine the role of local communities and sustainable livelihood practices in the effective management and restoration of wetland ecosystems.
articles.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with various bird species.
articles.trivia: India has the largest network of Ramsar Sites in South Asia.

articles.headline: India Exploring Regulatory Framework for Private Space Tourism Ventures
articles.explainer:
• The Indian government is reportedly drafting policies to facilitate and regulate private sector participation in space tourism.
• This move follows global trends in commercial spaceflight and aims to tap into the burgeoning space economy.
• Key considerations include safety standards, environmental impact assessments, and licensing procedures for private operators.
• ISRO's expertise and infrastructure could be leveraged to support these private ventures, fostering a robust domestic space industry.
• The policy aims to balance innovation with responsible development, positioning India as a future player in the global space tourism market.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Space technology
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors.
prelimsPoints: Space tourism involves private individuals traveling to space for recreational purposes.
ISRO is India's national space agency.
NewSpace India Limited (NSIL) is a public sector undertaking under ISRO, focused on commercializing space products and services.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential and challenges of developing a space tourism industry in India, considering its economic and technological implications.
Analyze the role of government policy and regulatory frameworks in fostering private sector participation in the space sector.
Examine the ethical and environmental considerations associated with the growth of space tourism globally.
articles.imageDescription: A conceptual image of a futuristic spacecraft designed for space tourism.
articles.trivia: The first private citizen to travel to space was Dennis Tito in 2001, aboard a Russian Soyuz spacecraft.

articles.headline: Major Cultural Heritage Restoration Projects Underway Across India
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) and state cultural departments are undertaking extensive restoration work on several historical monuments and sites.
• Projects focus on preserving architectural integrity, using traditional materials and techniques, and enhancing visitor experience.
• Funding comes from both central and state governments, often supplemented by corporate social responsibility (CSR) initiatives.
• The efforts align with India's commitment to safeguarding its rich cultural heritage and promoting cultural tourism.
• Challenges include managing environmental degradation, encroachment, and balancing conservation with tourism demands.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Conservation of cultural heritage.
prelimsPoints: Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage.
India has numerous UNESCO World Heritage Sites.
Restoration aims to preserve the authenticity and integrity of historical structures.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of cultural heritage conservation for national identity, tourism, and economic development in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by organizations like ASI in protecting and preserving India's vast and diverse cultural heritage.
Suggest innovative approaches and public-private partnerships for sustainable conservation and management of historical sites.
articles.imageDescription: Skilled artisans meticulously restoring intricate carvings on an ancient temple.
articles.trivia: The Ajanta Caves in Maharashtra are among India's earliest UNESCO World Heritage Sites, inscribed in 1983.

articles.headline: RBI's MPC Maintains Status Quo, Focuses on Inflation Moderation Amidst Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has decided to keep the repo rate unchanged in its latest review meeting.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach, balancing inflation control with supporting economic growth amidst global uncertainties.
• Food inflation remains a concern, influencing headline inflation figures, despite some moderation in core inflation.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth.
• Future policy actions will be guided by evolving economic data, particularly inflation trajectory and global commodity prices.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) determines the policy interest rates in India.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and functions of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in managing India's economy.
Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and supporting economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Examine the impact of monetary policy decisions on various sectors of the Indian economy.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India logo on a building facade.
articles.trivia: The MPC consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the government.

articles.headline: India Bolsters Maritime Security Capabilities Amidst Evolving Geopolitical Landscape
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Navy and Coast Guard are undertaking significant modernization and expansion efforts to enhance maritime security.
• Focus on acquiring advanced surveillance systems, naval vessels, and improving coastal defense infrastructure.
• Growing concerns over piracy, maritime terrorism, and geopolitical rivalries in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR) are driving these efforts.
• India is actively engaging in bilateral and multilateral maritime exercises with partner nations to enhance interoperability and regional security.
• The 'SAGAR' (Security and Growth for All in the Region) vision underlines India's commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: Indian Ocean Region (IOR) is strategically important for India.
SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region) is India's vision for the IOR.
Indian Navy and Coast Guard are key forces for maritime security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving maritime security challenges faced by India in the Indian Ocean Region and its strategic responses.
Analyze the importance of India's 'SAGAR' vision and its role in fostering regional stability and cooperation.
Examine the impact of naval modernization and international collaborations on India's capabilities to protect its maritime interests.
articles.imageDescription: An Indian Navy warship on patrol in the open sea.
articles.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 kilometers, including its island territories.

articles.headline: India's Tourism Sector Witnesses Strong Revival Post-Pandemic, Focus on Sustainable Practices
articles.explainer:
• The tourism sector in India is experiencing a robust recovery, with both domestic and international tourist arrivals showing significant growth.
• Government initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh' and 'Swadesh Darshan' are promoting domestic tourism and developing niche tourist circuits.
• Increased focus on sustainable tourism practices, responsible travel, and community-based tourism to minimize environmental impact.
• Digitalization of tourist services, enhanced connectivity, and improved infrastructure are contributing to the sector's growth.
• The revival is generating employment, boosting local economies, and showcasing India's diverse cultural and natural heritage.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: 'Dekho Apna Desh' is an initiative to promote domestic tourism.
'Swadesh Darshan' scheme aims at integrated development of theme-based tourist circuits.
Sustainable tourism focuses on minimizing negative impacts and maximizing benefits for local communities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of tourism in India's economic development and employment generation, especially in the post-pandemic era.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for promoting sustainable and responsible tourism practices in diverse regions of India.
Examine the effectiveness of government initiatives in boosting both domestic and international tourism and suggest further measures.
articles.imageDescription: A picturesque view of tourists enjoying a historical monument in India.
articles.trivia: The World Tourism Day is celebrated annually on September 27.

articles.headline: Climate Change Poses Growing Threat to Indian Agriculture, Adaptation Strategies Crucial
articles.explainer:
• Recent studies highlight the escalating impacts of climate change, including erratic rainfall, extreme weather events, and rising temperatures, on India's agricultural sector.
• Farmers are facing increased crop losses and reduced yields, threatening food security and rural livelihoods.
• Adaptation strategies such as climate-resilient crop varieties, precision agriculture, and improved irrigation techniques are becoming critical.
• Government policies are focusing on promoting sustainable farming practices and providing financial support to vulnerable farmers.
• International cooperation and technology transfer are essential for building agricultural resilience against climate shocks.
gsSubTopics: Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.

Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, – different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: India's agriculture is highly dependent on monsoon rainfall.
Climate change leads to extreme weather events like droughts and floods.
Climate-resilient crops are varieties that can withstand adverse climatic conditions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-dimensional impacts of climate change on Indian agriculture and its implications for food security and rural economy.
Discuss the various adaptation and mitigation strategies that can be implemented to build resilience in the agricultural sector.
Critically evaluate government policies and programs aimed at supporting farmers in the face of climate change and suggest areas for improvement.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer inspecting a field amidst changing weather patterns.
articles.trivia: About 55% of India's net sown area is rainfed, making it highly vulnerable to monsoon variability.

articles.headline: India's Foreign Trade Shows Resilience, Diversification Efforts Continue
articles.explainer:
• Latest trade data indicates continued resilience in India's foreign trade, with a focus on diversifying export markets and product baskets.
• Non-petroleum and non-gem & jewellery exports are showing encouraging trends, suggesting a broader base for growth.
• Government is actively pursuing Free Trade Agreements (FTAs) with key partners to enhance market access for Indian goods and services.
• Global economic slowdown and geopolitical tensions pose challenges, necessitating strategic trade policies.
• Emphasis on 'Make in India' and 'Vocal for Local' initiatives to boost domestic manufacturing and reduce import dependence.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: India aims to achieve $1 trillion in merchandise exports and $1 trillion in services exports by 2030.
FTA (Free Trade Agreement) reduces trade barriers between signatory countries.
'Make in India' initiative promotes domestic manufacturing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the current trends and challenges in India's foreign trade performance and its implications for economic growth.
Analyze the role of Free Trade Agreements (FTAs) and diversification strategies in enhancing India's export competitiveness.
Examine how initiatives like 'Make in India' contribute to reducing import dependence and strengthening India's position in global supply chains.
articles.imageDescription: Shipping containers stacked at a port, symbolizing global trade.
articles.trivia: India's largest trading partners include the USA, China, UAE, Saudi Arabia, and Iraq.

articles.headline: Review of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Highlights Progress and Roadblocks
articles.explainer:
• A mid-term review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation identifies areas of significant progress and persistent challenges.
• Progress noted in curriculum reforms, vocational education integration, and digital learning initiatives.
• Roadblocks include infrastructure gaps, teacher training requirements, and equitable access in remote areas.
• The policy aims for holistic and multidisciplinary education, skill development, and increased Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER).
• Stakeholder consultations are ongoing to refine implementation strategies and accelerate the transformation of India's education system.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
Key pillars include access, equity, quality, affordability, and accountability.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure instead of 10+2.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020 in addressing the challenges of India's education system.
Discuss the key successes and challenges encountered during the initial years of NEP 2020 implementation across different levels of education.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to quality education and effective integration of vocational training under the NEP framework.
articles.imageDescription: Students learning in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing education.
articles.trivia: The first National Policy on Education in India was formulated in 1968.

articles.headline: India Makes Strides in Quantum Technology Research and Development
articles.explainer:
• Indian scientific institutions and startups are reporting significant advancements in quantum computing, communication, and sensing.
• The National Quantum Mission (NQM) is providing substantial funding and strategic direction for indigenous R&D in this frontier technology.
• Focus areas include developing quantum processors, secure quantum communication networks, and advanced quantum sensors.
• Quantum technology holds immense potential for breakthroughs in computing power, cryptography, and medical imaging.
• International collaborations and talent development are crucial for India to emerge as a global leader in this rapidly evolving field.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: National Quantum Mission (NQM) was approved in April 2023.
Quantum computing uses principles of quantum mechanics.
Quantum communication offers inherently secure communication.
mainsPoints: Discuss the fundamental principles of quantum technology and its potential applications in various sectors.
Analyze the objectives and progress of India's National Quantum Mission in fostering indigenous research and development.
Examine the ethical considerations and geopolitical implications associated with the advancement of quantum technologies.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized representation of quantum bits (qubits) interacting.
articles.trivia: Unlike classical computers that use bits (0 or 1), quantum computers use qubits that can be 0, 1, or both simultaneously.

articles.headline: Government Initiatives Fuel Growth in India's Food Processing Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Food Processing Industries reports significant growth in the sector, driven by government schemes like PM Kisan Sampada Yojana and PLI schemes.
• Emphasis on reducing post-harvest losses, increasing value addition, and enhancing farmer incomes.
• Investments are flowing into cold chains, mega food parks, and modern processing infrastructure.
• The sector plays a crucial role in ensuring food security, generating employment, and boosting agricultural exports.
• Challenges include fragmented supply chains, lack of modern technology, and access to finance for small and medium enterprises.
gsSubTopics: Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: PM Kisan Sampada Yojana is a flagship scheme for the food processing sector.
PLI (Production Linked Incentive) schemes encourage domestic manufacturing.
Food processing reduces wastage and increases shelf life of agricultural produce.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the food processing sector for India's agricultural economy, rural development, and food security.
Analyze the impact of government schemes and policies in promoting growth and investment in the food processing industry.
Examine the challenges in integrating farmers with the food processing value chain and suggest measures for sustainable development of the sector.
articles.imageDescription: Processed food products displayed in a supermarket aisle.
articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest producers of several agricultural commodities, offering vast raw material for food processing.

articles.headline: Utilizing India's Demographic Dividend: Focus on Skilling and Employment Generation
articles.explainer:
• Discussions intensify on effectively harnessing India's large young population, often termed the 'demographic dividend', for economic growth.
• Emphasis on skill development programs, vocational training, and aligning education with industry demands to enhance employability.
• Addressing challenges of job creation, particularly in formal sectors, and promoting entrepreneurship among youth.
• Government initiatives like Skill India Mission and Start-up India aim to empower youth with necessary skills and opportunities.
• The success in utilizing this dividend will determine India's economic trajectory in the coming decades.
gsSubTopics: Population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Effects of globalization on Indian society.
Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism.
prelimsPoints: Demographic dividend refers to the economic growth potential that can result from shifts in a population's age structure.
India has one of the world's largest young populations.
Skill India Mission aims to train over 40 crore people in India in various skills.
mainsPoints: Analyze the opportunities and challenges associated with India's demographic dividend and its implications for economic development.
Discuss the effectiveness of current government policies and programs aimed at skilling and empowering the youth to leverage this dividend.
Suggest comprehensive strategies to ensure that India's large young population translates into sustained economic growth and social development.
articles.imageDescription: Young Indian professionals participating in a skill development workshop.
articles.trivia: India's working-age population is projected to be over 1 billion by 2030.

highlights: Tragedy strikes IAF Day celebrations in Chennai: Crowd crush claims multiple lives due to dehydration and stampede-like conditions.
India reviews impact of its G20 Presidency, focusing on sustainable development, climate finance, and digital public infrastructure.
Supreme Court to review ECI compliance on Electoral Bonds disclosure, reaffirming commitment to transparency in political funding.
India on track to achieve ambitious 500 GW renewable energy target by 2030, accelerating solar and wind projects.
ISRO intensifies preparations for Gaganyaan mission, India's maiden manned spaceflight program, with ongoing astronaut training and module testing.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is primarily responsible for overall disaster management in India, including policy formulation and planning?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) is the apex body for disaster management in India, headed by the Prime Minister. It is responsible for laying down policies, plans, and guidelines for disaster management.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEEC):
1. It was announced at the G7 Summit in 2023.
2. It aims to connect India, the Middle East, and Europe through a network of railways and shipping lanes.
3. It is seen as a counter to China's Belt and Road Initiative (BRI).
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. While the idea emerged from G7 discussions, IMEEC was formally announced at the G20 Summit in New Delhi in September 2023. Statements 2 and 3 are correct, as it aims to enhance connectivity and is seen as an alternative to BRI.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 is/are correct?
1. It replaced the National Policy on Education of 1992.
2. It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure.
3. It aims for 100% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in school education by 2030.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986 (modified in 1992). Statements 2 and 3 are correct as NEP 2020 proposes a new curricular structure and aims for 100% GER in school education by 2030.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's renewable energy targets, consider the following statements:
1. India aims to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
2. Solar and wind energy are the primary contributors to this target.
3. Green Hydrogen is a key area of focus for diversifying the renewable energy portfolio.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. India has set an ambitious target of achieving 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030, with solar and wind being major components. Green hydrogen is indeed being explored as a future renewable energy source.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is the primary objective of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI)?
options: (a) To manage the government's fiscal deficit.
(b) To maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
(d) To promote financial inclusion across the country.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The primary objective of the MPC is to maintain price stability (inflation targeting) while considering the objective of economic growth. Options (a), (c), and (d) are other functions or goals of the RBI, but not the primary objective of the MPC.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the factors contributing to crowd crush incidents at large public gatherings in India. Suggest comprehensive measures that authorities can implement to ensure public safety and prevent such tragedies. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Disaster Management; GS2: Governance, Government policies and interventions
mainsMocks.approach: Identify causes (poor planning, inadequate infrastructure, lack of coordination, public behavior, climate factors). Discuss effects (loss of life, economic impact, reputational damage). Suggest preventive measures: robust planning, crowd flow analysis, emergency services preparedness, public awareness, technology use, legal accountability, inter-agency coordination, training, and mock drills.

mainsMocks.question: India's G20 Presidency emphasized 'Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam' and championed the voice of the Global South. Evaluate the long-term implications of India's leadership on global governance and its strategic relevance. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India; Important International institutions
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's G20 theme and objectives. Discuss key achievements (AU inclusion, DPI, climate finance consensus, Global South voice). Analyze long-term implications: enhanced multilateralism, India's growing influence, shaping global agenda, challenges in implementation. Conclude on India's role as a bridge-builder and its strategic relevance.

mainsMocks.question: Despite significant progress in India's digital payments ecosystem, challenges related to cybersecurity, digital literacy, and infrastructure gaps persist. Discuss these challenges and suggest policy interventions to ensure inclusive and secure digital financial services. (10 marks, 150 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy (Inclusive growth, Science and Technology)
mainsMocks.approach: Acknowledge growth of digital payments (UPI). Outline challenges: cybersecurity threats, digital divide/illiteracy, infrastructure in remote areas, fraud. Suggest policy interventions: robust regulatory framework, public awareness campaigns, digital literacy programs, investment in secure infrastructure, grievance redressal mechanisms, collaboration with fintechs.

quickBytes.text: The Indian Air Force (IAF) was officially established on October 8, 1932.
Unified Payments Interface (UPI) is developed by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI).
The Ramsar Convention is an international treaty for the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.
India aims to achieve Net Zero emissions by the year 2070.
The term 'demographic dividend' refers to the economic growth potential from a favorable age structure.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is headed by the Governor of the Reserve Bank of India.
Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight mission, managed by ISRO.
The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is responsible for archaeological research and heritage protection.
India's National Logistics Policy (NLP) aims to reduce logistics costs as a percentage of GDP.
The 'JAM Trinity' refers to Jan Dhan accounts, Aadhaar, and Mobile numbers, promoting financial inclusion.

GS1.headline: Major Cultural Heritage Restoration Projects Underway Across India
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) and state cultural departments are undertaking extensive restoration work on several historical monuments and sites.
• Projects focus on preserving architectural integrity, using traditional materials and techniques, and enhancing visitor experience.
• Funding comes from both central and state governments, often supplemented by corporate social responsibility (CSR) initiatives.
• The efforts align with India's commitment to safeguarding its rich cultural heritage and promoting cultural tourism.
• Challenges include managing environmental degradation, encroachment, and balancing conservation with tourism demands.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Conservation of cultural heritage.
prelimsPoints: Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage.
India has numerous UNESCO World Heritage Sites.
Restoration aims to preserve the authenticity and integrity of historical structures.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of cultural heritage conservation for national identity, tourism, and economic development in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by organizations like ASI in protecting and preserving India's vast and diverse cultural heritage.
Suggest innovative approaches and public-private partnerships for sustainable conservation and management of historical sites.
GS1.imageDescription: Skilled artisans meticulously restoring intricate carvings on an ancient temple.
GS1.trivia: The Ajanta Caves in Maharashtra are among India's earliest UNESCO World Heritage Sites, inscribed in 1983.

GS1.headline: Utilizing India's Demographic Dividend: Focus on Skilling and Employment Generation
GS1.explainer:
• Discussions intensify on effectively harnessing India's large young population, often termed the 'demographic dividend', for economic growth.
• Emphasis on skill development programs, vocational training, and aligning education with industry demands to enhance employability.
• Addressing challenges of job creation, particularly in formal sectors, and promoting entrepreneurship among youth.
• Government initiatives like Skill India Mission and Start-up India aim to empower youth with necessary skills and opportunities.
• The success in utilizing this dividend will determine India's economic trajectory in the coming decades.
gsSubTopics: Population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Effects of globalization on Indian society.
Social empowerment, communalism, regionalism & secularism.
prelimsPoints: Demographic dividend refers to the economic growth potential that can result from shifts in a population's age structure.
India has one of the world's largest young populations.
Skill India Mission aims to train over 40 crore people in India in various skills.
mainsPoints: Analyze the opportunities and challenges associated with India's demographic dividend and its implications for economic development.
Discuss the effectiveness of current government policies and programs aimed at skilling and empowering the youth to leverage this dividend.
Suggest comprehensive strategies to ensure that India's large young population translates into sustained economic growth and social development.
GS1.imageDescription: Young Indian professionals participating in a skill development workshop.
GS1.trivia: India's working-age population is projected to be over 1 billion by 2030.

GS2.headline: India's G20 Presidency Legacy: A Year On, Assessing Impact and Future Trajectory
GS2.explainer:
• A comprehensive review is underway to assess the long-term impact and successful implementation of initiatives launched during India's G20 Presidency.
• Key focus areas include the New Delhi Leaders' Declaration, particularly commitments on sustainable development, climate finance, and digital public infrastructure.
• Discussions are centering on India's push for the inclusion of the African Union as a permanent G20 member and its implications for global governance.
• Experts are evaluating the effectiveness of the 'Voice of Global South Summit' in shaping the G20 agenda and promoting inclusive multilateralism.
• The review aims to identify enduring policy changes and frameworks that continue to guide global cooperation post-India's tenure.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: India held the G20 Presidency from December 1, 2022, to November 30, 2023.
The African Union became a permanent member of the G20 during India's presidency.
Key outcomes included the New Delhi Leaders' Declaration and emphasis on DPI.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the successes and challenges of India's G20 Presidency in advancing global cooperation and its national interests.
Discuss how India leveraged its G20 platform to champion the cause of the Global South and its implications for future international relations.
Analyze the significance of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) as a key deliverable of India's G20 Presidency and its potential for global replication.
GS2.imageDescription: Logo of the G20 with India's national flower, the lotus.
GS2.trivia: The G20 was formed in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis as a forum for finance ministers and central bank governors.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Review Compliance on Electoral Bonds Disclosure
GS2.explainer:
• A Supreme Court bench is scheduled to review the compliance reports submitted by the Election Commission of India (ECI) regarding the disclosure of electoral bond data.
• The court had earlier struck down the Electoral Bond Scheme as unconstitutional, citing violations of the right to information and voter's right to know.
• The review will ascertain if all directions, including the complete disclosure of donor and recipient details, have been adhered to by the State Bank of India (SBI) and ECI.
• This ongoing scrutiny emphasizes the judiciary's commitment to transparency in political funding and democratic processes.
• The outcome could further shape future regulations on political donations and electoral reforms.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: The Supreme Court declared the Electoral Bond Scheme unconstitutional in February 2024.
The ruling emphasized the Right to Information (Article 19(1)(a)) of voters.
State Bank of India (SBI) was directed to disclose details of electoral bonds to ECI.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on electoral bonds for transparency in political funding and democratic accountability in India.
Discuss the challenges in regulating political finance in India and suggest reforms to ensure fair and free elections.
Examine the role of the judiciary in upholding constitutional values and safeguarding fundamental rights in matters of electoral reforms.
GS2.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building facade.
GS2.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held from October 1951 to February 1952.

GS2.headline: India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEEC): Progress on Feasibility Studies
GS2.explainer:
• Feasibility studies for the ambitious India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEEC) are progressing, with initial reports indicating strong economic potential.
• The corridor, announced at the G20 Summit, aims to enhance connectivity through railway and shipping networks, bypassing traditional routes.
• It is envisioned to boost trade, energy flow, and digital connectivity between India, the Middle East, and Europe.
• Geopolitical implications are significant, offering an alternative to China's Belt and Road Initiative (BRI).
• Member countries are actively engaging in discussions to finalize funding mechanisms and project timelines.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: IMEEC was announced during the G20 Summit in New Delhi (2023).
It connects India, the Middle East, and Europe through sea and rail routes.
It is seen as part of the Partnership for Global Infrastructure and Investment (PGII).
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEEC) for India's foreign policy and trade ambitions.
Compare and contrast IMEEC with China's Belt and Road Initiative (BRI), highlighting their respective geopolitical implications.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in the successful implementation of such a mega infrastructure project involving multiple nations.
GS2.imageDescription: A map illustrating the proposed route of the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor.
GS2.trivia: The idea for IMEEC emerged from discussions at the G7 Summit in 2023, preceding its G20 announcement.

GS2.headline: Review of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Highlights Progress and Roadblocks
GS2.explainer:
• A mid-term review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation identifies areas of significant progress and persistent challenges.
• Progress noted in curriculum reforms, vocational education integration, and digital learning initiatives.
• Roadblocks include infrastructure gaps, teacher training requirements, and equitable access in remote areas.
• The policy aims for holistic and multidisciplinary education, skill development, and increased Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER).
• Stakeholder consultations are ongoing to refine implementation strategies and accelerate the transformation of India's education system.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry — the role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
Key pillars include access, equity, quality, affordability, and accountability.
It proposes a 5+3+3+4 curricular structure instead of 10+2.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020 in addressing the challenges of India's education system.
Discuss the key successes and challenges encountered during the initial years of NEP 2020 implementation across different levels of education.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to quality education and effective integration of vocational training under the NEP framework.
GS2.imageDescription: Students learning in a modern classroom setting, symbolizing education.
GS2.trivia: The first National Policy on Education in India was formulated in 1968.

GS3.headline: Tragedy Strikes IAF Day Celebrations in Chennai: Crowd Crush Claims Multiple Lives
GS3.explainer:
• At least five individuals lost their lives, and many others were hospitalized due to severe dehydration and injuries sustained in a crowd crush.
• The incident occurred during the 92nd-anniversary celebrations of the Indian Air Force at Tambaram Air Force Station in Chennai, Tamil Nadu.
• Overcrowding, inadequate facilities, and insufficient crowd management are being cited as primary reasons for the stampede-like situation.
• Authorities have initiated an inquiry into the incident to ascertain lapses in planning and execution of the large-scale public event.
• The event, meant to showcase IAF's prowess, turned tragic, highlighting critical issues in public event safety and disaster preparedness.
gsSubTopics: Disaster Management
Role of security forces and agencies
Government policies and interventions
prelimsPoints: Location of incident: Tambaram Air Force Station, Chennai, Tamil Nadu.
Event: 92nd anniversary celebration of the Indian Air Force.
Cause: Crowd crush leading to dehydration and injuries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges in crowd management during large public gatherings and suggest measures for effective disaster prevention.
Analyze the role of various stakeholders, including event organizers, local administration, and security forces, in ensuring public safety.
Examine the legal and ethical responsibilities in cases of public safety failures at government-organized events.
GS3.imageDescription: Emergency services responding to a crowd crush incident at a public event.
GS3.trivia: The Indian Air Force was officially established on October 8, 1932, as the Royal Indian Air Force.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Disaster Management
prelimsSyllabus.area: Disaster and disasters management in India
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Types of disasters and their management

GS3.headline: India on Track to Achieve Ambitious Renewable Energy Targets by 2030
GS3.explainer:
• Ministry of New and Renewable Energy reports significant progress towards India's target of 500 GW non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
• Recent policy initiatives and investment drives have accelerated solar and wind energy projects across the country.
• India's commitment to energy transition is crucial for meeting its Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) under the Paris Agreement.
• Challenges remain in grid integration, energy storage solutions, and land acquisition for large-scale projects.
• The government is exploring advanced technologies like green hydrogen and offshore wind to further diversify its renewable energy portfolio.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: India's target: 500 GW non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
Key renewable energy sources: Solar, Wind, Hydro.
India's NDCs are under the Paris Agreement.
mainsPoints: Discuss the opportunities and challenges for India in achieving its ambitious renewable energy targets by 2030.
Analyze the role of government policies and private sector investment in accelerating India's energy transition.
Examine the environmental and economic benefits of shifting towards a renewable energy-dominated power sector in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A large solar power farm with multiple photovoltaic panels.
GS3.trivia: India is the third-largest producer of renewable energy in the world.

GS3.headline: India's Digital Payments Ecosystem Continues Explosive Growth, Drives Financial Inclusion
GS3.explainer:
• Latest data indicates a sustained surge in digital payment transactions, particularly through UPI, reinforcing India's leadership in fintech.
• The growth is attributed to widespread adoption of smartphones, government initiatives like Jan Dhan-Aadhaar-Mobile (JAM) trinity, and innovative payment solutions.
• This expansion is significantly contributing to financial inclusion, bringing unbanked and underbanked populations into the formal financial system.
• Regulatory bodies are focusing on strengthening cybersecurity measures and ensuring robust infrastructure to support the increasing transaction volumes.
• The success of India's digital payment model is being studied globally for its potential to transform economies.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: UPI is a real-time payment system developed by NPCI.
JAM Trinity: Jan Dhan accounts, Aadhaar, Mobile numbers.
Digital payments contribute to financial inclusion.
mainsPoints: Analyze the role of digital payments, especially UPI, in driving financial inclusion and transforming India's economy.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities associated with the rapid expansion of India's digital payments ecosystem.
Examine how India's success in digital public infrastructure can be replicated in other developing nations.
GS3.imageDescription: A person making a payment using a smartphone with a UPI QR code.
GS3.trivia: India's UPI system processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month for the first time in August 2023.

GS3.headline: Gaganyaan Mission: ISRO Intensifies Preparations for Manned Spaceflight
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) is accelerating testing and training for the Gaganyaan mission, India's maiden manned spaceflight program.
• Key focus areas include astronaut training, development of life support systems, and validation of the crew module's re-entry capabilities.
• Recent uncrewed test flights have provided valuable data for refining mission parameters and ensuring crew safety.
• The mission aims to send Indian astronauts to low Earth orbit, marking a significant milestone in India's space endeavors.
• Collaboration with international space agencies continues for knowledge exchange and technical assistance.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Gaganyaan is India's first manned space mission.
ISRO is the agency responsible for the mission.
The mission aims to send astronauts to Low Earth Orbit (LEO).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance and scientific objectives of India's Gaganyaan mission.
Analyze the technological challenges and indigenous capabilities developed for the successful execution of manned spaceflight.
Examine the socio-economic benefits and international standing gained through ambitious space programs like Gaganyaan.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of the Gaganyaan crew module orbiting Earth.
GS3.trivia: The first Indian to travel to space was Rakesh Sharma in 1984, aboard the Soviet Soyuz T-11.

GS3.headline: National Logistics Policy: Driving Efficiency and Reducing Costs in India's Supply Chain
GS3.explainer:
• The National Logistics Policy (NLP), launched in 2022, is showing tangible results in streamlining India's logistics sector.
• Efforts are focused on integrating digital systems, developing multi-modal transportation, and creating a unified regulatory framework.
• The policy aims to reduce logistics costs as a percentage of GDP, making Indian goods more competitive globally.
• Infrastructure projects like the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan are crucial enablers for the NLP's success.
• Improved logistics efficiency is expected to boost manufacturing, exports, and create new employment opportunities.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: National Logistics Policy (NLP) was launched in September 2022.
Aim: Reduce logistics cost as a percentage of GDP.
PM Gati Shakti is a complementary initiative for integrated infrastructure development.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and key pillars of the National Logistics Policy and its potential to transform India's economic landscape.
Analyze how improved logistics can enhance India's manufacturing competitiveness and boost its position in global supply chains.
Examine the challenges in implementing a comprehensive logistics policy and suggest measures for effective coordination among various stakeholders.
GS3.imageDescription: A container ship being loaded at a modern port, symbolizing logistics.
GS3.trivia: India's logistics cost is estimated to be around 13-14% of its GDP, higher than the global average of 8-10%.

GS3.headline: Delhi Government Unveils Winter Action Plan to Combat Air Pollution
GS3.explainer:
• The Delhi government has announced a comprehensive action plan to tackle severe air pollution anticipated during the upcoming winter months.
• Measures include strict enforcement against stubble burning, vehicular emissions control, dust management, and industrial pollution monitoring.
• Focus on promoting public transport and electric vehicles, alongside green crackers during festivals.
• Inter-state cooperation with neighboring states like Punjab and Haryana remains crucial for addressing transboundary pollution sources.
• Public awareness campaigns and citizen participation are integral to the success of the anti-pollution efforts.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Key sources of air pollution in Delhi in winter: Stubble burning, vehicular emissions, dust, industrial pollution.
GRAP (Graded Response Action Plan) is implemented in Delhi-NCR to tackle air pollution.
CAQM (Commission for Air Quality Management) oversees air quality in NCR.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges in combating air pollution in Delhi-NCR and critically evaluate the effectiveness of current policy interventions.
Analyze the role of inter-state cooperation and technological solutions in addressing transboundary pollution issues like stubble burning.
Suggest sustainable long-term strategies for improving urban air quality, considering economic and social implications.
GS3.imageDescription: A smoggy cityscape of Delhi during winter, highlighting air pollution.
GS3.trivia: Delhi often ranks among the most polluted capital cities in the world, especially during winter.

GS3.headline: India's Semiconductor Manufacturing Ambitions Gain Momentum with New Investments
GS3.explainer:
• Several new proposals for semiconductor fabrication units and assembly, testing, marking, and packaging (ATMP) facilities are under government review.
• The 'India Semiconductor Mission' (ISM) is providing significant incentives and support to attract global players and foster indigenous capabilities.
• Aims to establish India as a global hub for semiconductor design and manufacturing, reducing reliance on imports.
• Challenges include capital intensity, technology transfer complexities, and skilled workforce availability.
• The strategic importance of semiconductors for national security and economic growth is driving this push.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
prelimsPoints: India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) is a dedicated program to promote semiconductor manufacturing.
ATMP refers to Assembly, Testing, Marking, and Packaging, a critical part of chip manufacturing.
Semiconductors are essential components for modern electronics and digital infrastructure.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic imperative for India to develop indigenous semiconductor manufacturing capabilities and the challenges involved.
Analyze the effectiveness of government policies, such as the India Semiconductor Mission, in attracting investments and fostering a semiconductor ecosystem.
Examine the potential impact of a robust domestic semiconductor industry on India's economic growth, technological sovereignty, and national security.
GS3.imageDescription: A close-up of a silicon wafer used in semiconductor manufacturing.
GS3.trivia: Globally, Taiwan and South Korea dominate the advanced semiconductor manufacturing market.

GS3.headline: New Framework Proposed for Integrated Wetland Conservation and Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change is finalizing a new policy framework for the holistic conservation and management of India's wetlands.
• The framework emphasizes community participation, sustainable utilization of wetland resources, and integration with local development plans.
• Wetlands are vital ecosystems providing numerous services, including water purification, flood control, and biodiversity support.
• India is a signatory to the Ramsar Convention, which focuses on the conservation and wise use of wetlands.
• The initiative aims to protect and restore these critical habitats which are under increasing threat from urbanization and pollution.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and Environment.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Wetlands are transition zones between terrestrial and aquatic systems.
The Ramsar Convention (1971) is an international treaty for wetland conservation.
India has designated numerous Ramsar Sites, which are Wetlands of International Importance.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of wetlands and the major threats they face in India.
Critically analyze the existing policy and legal framework for wetland conservation in India and suggest improvements.
Examine the role of local communities and sustainable livelihood practices in the effective management and restoration of wetland ecosystems.
GS3.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with various bird species.
GS3.trivia: India has the largest network of Ramsar Sites in South Asia.

GS3.headline: India Exploring Regulatory Framework for Private Space Tourism Ventures
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian government is reportedly drafting policies to facilitate and regulate private sector participation in space tourism.
• This move follows global trends in commercial spaceflight and aims to tap into the burgeoning space economy.
• Key considerations include safety standards, environmental impact assessments, and licensing procedures for private operators.
• ISRO's expertise and infrastructure could be leveraged to support these private ventures, fostering a robust domestic space industry.
• The policy aims to balance innovation with responsible development, positioning India as a future player in the global space tourism market.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Space technology
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors.
prelimsPoints: Space tourism involves private individuals traveling to space for recreational purposes.
ISRO is India's national space agency.
NewSpace India Limited (NSIL) is a public sector undertaking under ISRO, focused on commercializing space products and services.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential and challenges of developing a space tourism industry in India, considering its economic and technological implications.
Analyze the role of government policy and regulatory frameworks in fostering private sector participation in the space sector.
Examine the ethical and environmental considerations associated with the growth of space tourism globally.
GS3.imageDescription: A conceptual image of a futuristic spacecraft designed for space tourism.
GS3.trivia: The first private citizen to travel to space was Dennis Tito in 2001, aboard a Russian Soyuz spacecraft.

GS3.headline: RBI's MPC Maintains Status Quo, Focuses on Inflation Moderation Amidst Global Headwinds
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) has decided to keep the repo rate unchanged in its latest review meeting.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach, balancing inflation control with supporting economic growth amidst global uncertainties.
• Food inflation remains a concern, influencing headline inflation figures, despite some moderation in core inflation.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth.
• Future policy actions will be guided by evolving economic data, particularly inflation trajectory and global commodity prices.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary Policy.
prelimsPoints: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) determines the policy interest rates in India.
Repo rate is the rate at which RBI lends money to commercial banks.
RBI's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role and functions of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in managing India's economy.
Analyze the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control and supporting economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Examine the impact of monetary policy decisions on various sectors of the Indian economy.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India logo on a building facade.
GS3.trivia: The MPC consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the government.

GS3.headline: India Bolsters Maritime Security Capabilities Amidst Evolving Geopolitical Landscape
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Navy and Coast Guard are undertaking significant modernization and expansion efforts to enhance maritime security.
• Focus on acquiring advanced surveillance systems, naval vessels, and improving coastal defense infrastructure.
• Growing concerns over piracy, maritime terrorism, and geopolitical rivalries in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR) are driving these efforts.
• India is actively engaging in bilateral and multilateral maritime exercises with partner nations to enhance interoperability and regional security.
• The 'SAGAR' (Security and Growth for All in the Region) vision underlines India's commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: Indian Ocean Region (IOR) is strategically important for India.
SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region) is India's vision for the IOR.
Indian Navy and Coast Guard are key forces for maritime security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving maritime security challenges faced by India in the Indian Ocean Region and its strategic responses.
Analyze the importance of India's 'SAGAR' vision and its role in fostering regional stability and cooperation.
Examine the impact of naval modernization and international collaborations on India's capabilities to protect its maritime interests.
GS3.imageDescription: An Indian Navy warship on patrol in the open sea.
GS3.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 kilometers, including its island territories.

GS3.headline: India's Tourism Sector Witnesses Strong Revival Post-Pandemic, Focus on Sustainable Practices
GS3.explainer:
• The tourism sector in India is experiencing a robust recovery, with both domestic and international tourist arrivals showing significant growth.
• Government initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh' and 'Swadesh Darshan' are promoting domestic tourism and developing niche tourist circuits.
• Increased focus on sustainable tourism practices, responsible travel, and community-based tourism to minimize environmental impact.
